news.title: Notandas Realty strengthens its legacy of craftsmanship with ultra-modern luxury innovation
news.para: Notandas Realty has carved a niche as a top-tier luxury developer within Mumbai’s fast-paced real estate landscape. Boasting close to two decades of industry insight, the company specializes in stunning homes and high-end commercial areas. Guided by the visionary Mr. Harsh Jagwani, Notandas Realty leverages modern technology to ensure superior service and operational excellence.

news.title: Birla Estates, Arvind SmartSpaces enter Mumbai’s redevelopment segment with new housing projects
news.para: Birla Estates and Arvind SmartSpaces are expanding into Mumbai's property market. They have launched new redevelopment projects in Khar West and Santacruz. These ventures tap into the city's demand for housing. These projects represent significant revenue potential for both companies.

news.title: Madhuri Dixit leases commercial property in Mumbai for ₹2.81 crore
news.para: Bollywood star Madhuri Dixit has leased a commercial space in Mumbai's One Lodha Place for a substantial monthly rent starting at ₹4.25 lakh. The five-year agreement, registered in March 2026, includes a 67.91 sq meter unit and parking.  Rent escalates annually, culminating in a total lease value of ₹2.81 crore over the term.

news.title: Telangana high court refuses stay on Bhoodan land demolitions
news.para: Telangana High Court refused to halt demolitions and rehabilitation at Bhoodan lands in Khammam, citing potential harm to eligible poor beneficiaries. The court noted that some affected families have accepted allotted plots, while others are still under consideration. The state government was directed to file a detailed counter-affidavit.

news.title: At ₹4,047 crore, property tax receipts in Bengaluru 40% short of ₹6,700-crore target
news.para: Bengaluru's property tax collections have fallen significantly short of the Rs 6,700 crore target for the 2025-26 fiscal, realizing only Rs 4,047.7 crore, a 60% collection rate. This underperformance, despite intensified enforcement including property attachments and auctions, highlights persistent compliance gaps. The new financial year will see corporations directly accountable for revenue mobilization.

news.title: Kolhapur civic body announces 80% rebate on property tax and water bill penalties
news.para: Kolhapur Municipal Corporation offers a significant 80 percent discount on property tax penalties and water bill late fees. This special scheme is available for defaulters who clear all outstanding dues and the current bill in one payment. The offer is valid from March 26 to March 31, 2026.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh cabinet approves draft of Amaravati capital resolution
news.para: Andhra Pradesh cabinet has approved a draft resolution to be presented in the assembly, seeking an amendment to the State Reorganisation Act to legally establish Amaravati as the sole capital. This move aims to prevent future regime changes from altering the capital.

news.title: UP-RERA amends regulations, allows complaints in unregistered projects; caps transfer charges
news.para: UP-RERA has introduced significant changes to its regulations effective March 25, 2026. Homebuyers in unregistered projects can now approach UP-RERA for grievance redressal. The authority will determine if registration was mandatory and take necessary action. Additionally, caps have been placed on administrative charges for property transfers. These amendments aim to enhance transparency and efficiency for consumers.

news.title: With vertical growth focus, ordinance planned for apartment residents in Karnataka
news.para: Karnataka is set to enact the Karnataka Apartment Ownership and Management Act 2025 (KAOMA) via ordinance soon, signaling a shift towards vertical growth due to land constraints. This move, welcomed by apartment residents and federations, aims to enhance transparency and accountability for millions living in apartments across the state.

news.title: Prestige Group and Arihant Group jointly acquire land in Padi, Chennai
news.para: Prestige Group and Arihant Group have joined forces to acquire a significant land parcel in Padi, Chennai. This joint venture will develop a large residential project. The development is expected to offer substantial saleable area and generate considerable revenue. This expansion signifies a commitment to high-quality housing in the city.

news.title: House photo will not be required for property tax payment: Mohali mayor
news.para: Mohali Municipal Corporation will soon withdraw the requirement of submitting house photographs for property tax deposits following resident and councillor concerns. This move aims to boost tax collection, which has seen a 15% deficit. The MC also approved increasing sanitation staff and regularizing long-serving workers to manage expanded municipal limits.

news.title: Glada demolishes 13 buildings in Ludhiana, tears up roads in three colonies
news.para: Ludhiana authorities demolished 13 unauthorized buildings and dismantled three illegal housing colonies in a major enforcement drive. The action targets developers who bypassed government norms, with Glada vowing to continue these operations. Buyers are warned that illegal plots will receive no municipal services and face legal risks.

news.title: CAG report flags cases of dual benefits under housing schemes in Gujarat
news.para: The CAG has found instances where individuals received benefits from more than one government housing scheme. This is against the rules. In Jamnagar and Surat districts, 24 beneficiaries were sanctioned houses under both PMAY-G and PMAY-Urban. Seven of these received significant financial aid from both schemes.

news.title: Delhi Police EOW books entities of Gurugram-based Experion Developers for fraud
news.para: Delhi Police's Economic Offences Wing has filed an FIR against Experion Developers and Experion Capital for fraud, stemming from an ED investigation into Religare Finvest. The probe revealed the Singh brothers allegedly siphoned Rs 2,036 crore from RFL, with Rs 150 crore used to buy land in Gurugram.

news.title: Pune civic body restricts construction and excavation activities to 14 hours a day
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation has restricted construction and excavation activities to 14 hours daily, from 7 am to 9 pm, following resident complaints about pollution. The directive aims to address health-related issues caused by construction noise and air pollution. The civic body will take action against violators, including stop-work orders.

news.title: Madras HC issues notice to Tamil Nadu on PIL against layout approvals in hill areas
news.para: An activist has approached the Madras High Court challenging a Tamil Nadu government order. This order empowers urban planning authorities to approve housing layouts in hill areas. It bypasses the mandatory clearance from the Hill Area Conservation Authority (HACA). The petitioner argues this dilutes environmental safeguards and could cause ecological damage.

news.title: Under smart city mission, over 357 projects worth over ₹11,000 crore completed in Gujarat
news.para: With an extensive network of roads and railways, along with the development of international airports and ports, Gujarat today has a well-developed transport network that provides excellent connectivity to its citizens. To manage the increasing traffic in urban areas efficiently, overbridges and underbridges are being constructed across the state. In particular, in the four major cities, Ahmedabad, Rajkot, Vadodara, and Surat, a strong road network is being developed through such infrastructure, making transportation easier for citizens.

news.title: About 34% of 72 lakh Karnataka homes don't have tap water: CAG
news.para: Karnataka has fallen significantly short of its tap water connection targets under the Jal Jeevan Mission, with 24.5 lakh rural households still lacking access past the March 2024 deadline. A CAG report highlights major planning, execution, and financial management flaws, impacting water quality monitoring and project completion.

news.title: Lucknow development body to launch OTS scheme, set up desk to help defaulters
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority launches a one-time settlement scheme from April 18, offering a waiver of penal interest and a 2% rebate for full payment within 30 days. A dedicated help desk will assist applicants, with applications to be processed within three months. The scheme covers various property types, including residential, commercial, and institutional allotments.

news.title: HYDRAA reviews layout violations & drainage issues in Sangareddy district of Telangana
news.para: HYDRAA conducted field inspections in Sangareddy district following Prajavani complaints on encroachments and infrastructure issues. Officials reviewed alleged illegal occupation of public spaces in Anand Nagar layout and Beeramguda, and inspected Shambunikunta lake for flooding concerns, vowing to verify records and take necessary action.
